seoTitle: Windsor Downs vs Winegrove — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Windsor Downs and Winegrove. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Windsor Downs vs Winegrove: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Windsor Downs and Winegrove.
heading: Windsor Downs vs Winegrove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Windsor Downs, New South Wales and Winegrove, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Winegrove has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Windsor Downs (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Windsor Downs or Winegrove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Windsor Downs has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Winegrove scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Windsor Downs scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Winegrove scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Windsor Downs or Winegrove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Winegrove has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
